config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan dan keputusan mayoritas yang semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, sering kali alasan "demi kepentingan umum" dipakai untuk menekan suara yang lemah atau minoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang bisa menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama dengan perlindungan hak tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, that idea sets off warning bells because history here shows how easily “the common good” can be used to justify discrimination, censorship, or abuse of power, especially against minorities. Societies do need rules, taxes, public health measures, and other collective sacrifices, but those should be limited by strong individual rights, not allowed to erase them. A healthy society protects the common good by respecting basic rights first, not by treating the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk mengalahkan hak individu secara otomatis. Di Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya besar ketika frasa seperti “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menggusur orang kecil, atau menekan minoritas tanpa perlindungan yang layak. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar individu supaya kekuasaan, baik dari negara maupun mayoritas, tidak semena-mena. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan kebutuhan umum dengan martabat dan hak setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of statement is dangerous because it can turn into a blank check for governments or majorities to silence dissent, punish unpopular groups, or justify unfair treatment in the name of the common good. I do think society sometimes has to ask individuals to accept limits, like laws, taxes, or public health rules, but those limits should be narrow and accountable, not a reason to erase basic rights. In my view, a society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and then builds shared responsibilities around them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman kritik, penggusuran, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil, jadi bagi saya itu berbahaya kalau dijadikan prinsip utama. Kepentingan bersama memang perlu, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar supaya negara dan mayoritas tidak bertindak semena-mena. Masyarakat yang sehat justru yang mampu menyeimbangkan kebutuhan umum dengan perlindungan martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual. As an American, I am very wary of that idea because it can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or abuse of power whenever leaders claim they are acting for the "greater good." Society does need collective rules and sacrifices, but those are legitimate only when they are constrained by strong individual rights and real accountability. In my view, a free society is not one that treats the individual as expendable, but one that protects basic rights precisely so the common good cannot become a cover for oppression.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
